description: A designed DHS 'National Immigrant Heritage Month' spotlight card with a deep navy-blue background featuring a faint, large American flag (stars and stripes) as a watermark/texture. The white bold headline at top reads 'DHS Celebrates National Immigrant Heritage Month.' Centered is a rectangular official-style portrait of a man in a dark CBP (Customs and Border Protection) uniform with a badge, standing in front of an American flag and a second flag, name tape partly visible. Below a large white quotation mark is the testimonial in white serif text: "My entire life in the U.S. is dedicated to showing my gratitude for the opportunity to live in the greatest country on Earth. Every day, I am thankful for the chance and the green card we received in 2013. I have achieved a lot and aim to achieve even more, contributing to making the U.S. stronger and more resilient." A blue rounded banner at bottom-left gives the attribution: 'Yahor S. / CBP, Customs and Border Protection Officer / 1st generation from Republic of Belarus.' The circular DHS seal sits at bottom-right. This is patriotic workforce-spotlight / pro-immigration-heritage messaging produced under the prior administration, humanizing a foreign-born federal officer; target audience is the public and DHS workforce, emphasizing gratitude, assimilation and the 'American dream.'
notable_text: DHS Celebrates National Immigrant Heritage Month; "My entire life in the U.S. is dedicated to showing my gratitude for the opportunity to live in the greatest country on Earth..."; Yahor S., CBP, Customs and Border Protection Officer, 1st generation from Republic of Belarus
